name: Safe C++ (P3390 / Circle)
summary: Sean Baxter's Safe C++ grafts Rust-style affine types and an invasive borrow checker onto C++ as a `#feature on safety` mode with a parallel `std2` standard library. Implemented end-to-end in Circle (single-author Clang fork); effectively dead in WG21 as of mid-2025 after SG23 voted to prioritize Profiles.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Safe C++ (Sean Baxter, P3390) grafts Rust-style affine types plus an invasive borrow checker onto C++ as a `#feature on safety` mode with a parallel `std2` standard library. The proposal includes a viral `safe`-specifier and full Mid-Level IR-based NLL borrow checking. New reference syntax `T^` / `const T^` (mutable/shared borrow) with explicit lifetime parameters and outlives-constraints; `rel` relocation operator; choice types (Rust-style enums) with safe pattern matching; and affine (move-only) value semantics enforcing the law of exclusivity.

sections.title: P3444 — Memory safety without lifetime parameters
sections.content: P3444 is an alternative formulation ("Memory Safety without Lifetime Parameters") that uses `T%` reference with a single invented lifetime per call — eliminates explicit lifetime annotation burden at cost of expressivity. The trade is that callers and callees can no longer relate borrow lifetimes through the type system; the analysis becomes a simpler intra-procedural check at the cost of rejecting some legitimate patterns that explicit lifetimes would express.

sources.label: P3444 Memory Safety without Lifetime Parameters

sections.title: Circle implementation and WG21 status
sections.content:
Implemented end-to-end in Circle (single-author Clang fork); proves a borrow-checker-equipped C++ subset is feasible. Circle is the only C++ compiler that has implemented full NLL-style borrow checking on a real C++ codebase, and the implementation is widely cited as evidence that the Safe C++ design is technically achievable.

Status (as of 2026-04): effectively dead in WG21; Baxter publicly stopped work mid-2025 ("The Rust safety model is unpopular with the committee"). His critique site enumerates concrete reasons Profiles cannot achieve sound memory safety without aliasing information — the technical position is that no analysis without viral aliasing/exclusivity annotation can be sound, only heuristic.

sources.label: P3444 Memory Safety without Lifetime Parameters
